aliases: Black tea with evaporated milk no sugar

english: Black tea with evaporated milk no sugar

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'tea beverage' class; no specific entry for Teh C Kosong (black tea with evaporated milk, unsweetened). Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers black tea infusion. Teh C Kosong includes evaporated milk but no specific composite FoodEx2 code exists; black tea infusion used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for Teh C Kosong. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for regional hawker beverages.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Teh C Kosong is approximately one cup (200 ml / 200 g), as typically served at a Singapore hawker centre or kopitiam.

nutrient_source.title: Teh C Kosong — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id D09020068 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status 'reviewed'. All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this source. Extended micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Teh C Kosong. GI estimated at 35 based on black tea with milk (unsweetened) analogous entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Black tea has negligible carbohydrate contribution; the small lactose contribution from evaporated milk (GI ~46) at this dilution yields an estimated composite GI of approximately 35, consistent with low-GI classification. GL per 200 g serving = (35 × 2.56 × 2.0) / 100 = 1.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (26 mg/100 g) and no added sugar
hypertension.guidance: Teh C Kosong is suitable for individuals with hypertension; its sodium content of 26 mg/100 g is well below the threshold of concern, and the absence of added sugar means it does not contribute to sugar-driven blood pressure elevation — confirm with the hawker that no sugar is added at preparation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low total carbohydrate (2.56 g/100 g), no added sugar, low estimated GI (~35)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Teh C Kosong poses minimal glycaemic impact for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, with only 2.56 g total carbohydrate per 100 g (approximately 5.1 g per 200 ml serving), all from naturally occurring lactose in evaporated milk — patients should confirm no sugar is added at the point of preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.1 g/100 g), no added sugar, black tea polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Teh C Kosong is appropriate for individuals managing cardiovascular disease; the saturated fat from evaporated milk is low at typical serving dilution (~2.2 g per 200 ml serving), sodium is minimal, and black tea polyphenols (theaflavins, thearubigins) have documented endothelial and anti-inflammatory benefits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory black tea polyphenols, no added sugar, no trans fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Teh C Kosong is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; black tea polyphenols (theaflavins and thearubigins) have anti-inflammatory properties, and the absence of added sugar and trans fat avoids pro-inflammatory dietary triggers.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh C Kosong is a traditional Singaporean kopitiam (coffee shop) beverage consisting of strong brewed black tea blended with evaporated milk and served without any added sugar. The name derives from Hokkien and Malay: 'Teh' means tea, 'C' refers to evaporated milk (Carnation brand historically), and 'Kosong' means empty or zero in Malay, signifying no sugar. It is a staple of Singapore hawker culture, available at virtually every kopitiam and hawker centre, and is consumed at breakfast, lunch, and throughout the day. The beverage has a creamy, mildly astringent flavour profile with the characteristic tannin bitterness of Ceylon or Assam black tea softened by the richness of evaporated milk. Per 100 g, it provides 30 kcal, 1.17 g protein, 1.66 g fat, and 2.56 g carbohydrate, with 26 mg sodium and 0.85 g dietary fibre. The sugar content of 1.66 g/100 g is entirely from naturally occurring lactose in the evaporated milk — no sugar is added. This makes it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease, provided no sugar is added at preparation. Black tea polyphenols including theaflavins and thearubigins contribute anti-inflammatory and cardioprotective properties. The estimated glycemic index is approximately 35 (low), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 1.8 per standard 200 ml serving.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id D09020068, review_status 'reviewed', confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. The food is an exact match in the HPB database. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2021, clearly documented.
notes: Saturated fat estimated at 1.08 g/100 g based on the known fatty acid profile of evaporated milk at the dilution implied by the HPB per_100g fat value of 1.66 g; HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient and fatty acid fractions are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for Teh C Kosong (D09020068) and are not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific composite beverage as of 2026. The 'keto' dietary tag from the input has been retained in meal_context as contextually plausible given the low carbohydrate content (2.56 g/100 g), but is not formally asserted as a dietary flag without clinical validation. 'Kosong' preparation can vary at hawker stalls; consumers should confirm no sugar is added at point of service.
